pages.text:
CharCoat SC
AUTHOR SEAN RITCHIE
VERSION 2.0
PRODUCT DESCRIPTION
PURPOSE/CHANGES TECHNICAL
CharCoat SC Structural Steel Fire Protection is the pinnacle of DATE PUBLISHED JUNE 2026
technology and performance in Thin Film Intumescent Fireproofing.
SC can be applied to steelwork in interior or covered exterior appli- TECHNICAL DATA
cations. SC fireproofing coating is spray applied and offers up to 150
Composition Low VOC, Waterbased, NON-DG
minutes fire protection under UL263 and BS476: Part 21. There is no
Certification BS476, Part 21 | UL 263 | GB14907-2018
reinforcement or mesh required.
Building Classification C1 and C2 Environments
Specific Gravity 1.30 ± 0.01kg/L (25oC)
SC can be applied in residential, commercial and industrial applica-
Volume Solids 69% ± 3%
tions where architectural designs call for a thin-film, aesthetic,
VOC 1 g/L
highly decorative finish. Theoretical Coverage 1800g/m² based on an applied @ 1.0mm DFT
Colour White / matte
Semi exterior or covered areas can also be completed using a Primer Epoxy Zinc Primer or similar
urethane, epoxy or water based topcoat to seal the fireproofing Tocoat Water-based topcoats (interior if required)
system. With SC’s low spread rate, higher than average solids and Drying Time at 25oC 2 hours (touch) 4 hours (hard dry) 72 hours full cure
extremely low DFT’s, we can provide the most cost effective Coating Intervals at 25oC Spray min. 4-6 hours
solution for all of your steel fireproofing needs. PLEASE CONTACT US FOR OTHER PROPERTIES
PACKAGING
• 25 kg Drum
• Plastic pails
FEATURES AND BENEFITS
• Other sizes on request
• Fire rates steelwork up to 150 minutes
TRANSPORT / STORAGE
• Low VOC
• 20 to 30 times typical intumescent expansion Transport and storage free from frost- preferably at a minimum of
• Extremely low DFT’s +5°C to a maximum of +35°C.
• Water based intumescent coatings
SHELF LIFE
• Non-Toxic / Non-Hazardous (water based)
• Most steelwork 1 or 2 coat application When stored at the recommended conditions, unopened pails have
• Permanent cost effective solution a shelf life of 12 months from date of manufacture.
• Zero maintenance required
• Single component (stir and spray) APPLICATION GUIDELINES
• Brush application also available For Interior applications, use SC (up to 150 minutes). Topcoats are
not required for interior works, but may be needed simply for
colour. For covered external works, topcoats are required. Water
APPLICATION AREAS
based topcoats, urethane or epoxy topcoats are acceptable. SC will
• Airports • Atriums last the life time of the steelwork if properly maintained.
• Stadiums and • Warehousing
Leisure Facilities • Powerstations GUARANTEE / WARRANTY
• Office Buildings • Mines Please refer to our full Terms and Conditions of sale.
• Retail Complexes • Smelters
• Cinemas • Shipyards No liability can be accepted for the information provided in this
• Residential Low and • Swimming Pools
document although it is published in good faith and believed to be
Highrise Apartments (C4 topcoat) correct. CharCoat Passive Fire Protection reserves the right to alter
product specifications without prior notice, in line with Company
policy of continuous development and improvement.

rows: CharCoat SC AUTHOR SEAN RITCHIE VERSION 2.0 PRODUCT DESCRIPTION PURPOSE/CHANGES TECHNICAL CharCoat SC Structural Steel Fire Protection is the pinnacle of DATE PUBLISHED JUNE 2026 technology and performance in Thin Film Intumescent Fireproofing. SC can be applied to steelwork in interior or covered exterior appli- TECHNICAL DATA cations. SC fireproofing coating is spray applied and offers up to 150 Composition Low VOC, Waterbased, NON-DG minutes fire protection under UL263 and BS476: Part 21. There is no Certification BS476, Part 21 | UL 263 | GB14907-2018 reinforcement or mesh required. Building Classification C1 and C2 Environments Specific Gravity 1.30 ± 0.01kg/L (25oC) SC can be applied in residential, commercial and industrial applica- Volume Solids 69% ± 3% tions where architectural designs call for a thin-film, aesthetic, VOC 1 g/L highly decorative finish. Theoretical Coverage 1800g/m² based on an applied @ 1.0mm DFT Colour White / matte Semi exterior or covered areas can also be completed using a Primer Epoxy Zinc Primer or similar urethane, epoxy or water based topcoat to seal the fireproofing Tocoat Water-based topcoats (interior if required) system. With SC’s low spread rate, higher than average solids and Drying Time at 25oC 2 hours (touch) 4 hours (hard dry) 72 hours full cure extremely low DFT’s, we can provide the most cost effective Coating Intervals at 25oC Spray min. 4-6 hours solution for all of your steel fireproofing needs. PLEASE CONTACT US FOR OTHER PROPERTIES PACKAGING • 25 kg Drum • Plastic pails FEATURES AND BENEFITS • Other sizes on request • Fire rates steelwork up to 150 minutes TRANSPORT / STORAGE • Low VOC • 20 to 30 times typical intumescent expansion Transport and storage free from frost- preferably at a minimum of • Extremely low DFT’s +5°C to a maximum of +35°C. • Water based intumescent coatings SHELF LIFE • Non-Toxic / Non-Hazardous (water based) • Most steelwork 1 or 2 coat application When stored at the recommended conditions, unopened pails have • Permanent cost effective solution a shelf life of 12 months from date of manufacture. • Zero maintenance required • Single component (stir and spray) APPLICATION GUIDELINES • Brush application also available For Interior applications, use SC (up to 150 minutes). Topcoats are not required for interior works, but may be needed simply for colour. For covered external works, topcoats are required. Water APPLICATION AREAS based topcoats, urethane or epoxy topcoats are acceptable. SC will • Airports • Atriums last the life time of the steelwork if properly maintained. • Stadiums and • Warehousing Leisure Facilities • Powerstations GUARANTEE / WARRANTY • Office Buildings • Mines Please refer to our full Terms and Conditions of sale. • Retail Complexes • Smelters • Cinemas • Shipyards No liability can be accepted for the information provided in this • Residential Low and • Swimming Pools document although it is published in good faith and believed to be Highrise Apartments (C4 topcoat) correct. CharCoat Passive Fire Protection reserves the right to alter product specifications without prior notice, in line with Company policy of continuous development and improvement.
AUTHOR SEAN RITCHIE VERSION 2.0 PURPOSE/CHANGES TECHNICAL DATE PUBLISHED JUNE 2026

rows: 2 hours (touch) 4 hours (hard dry) 72 hours full cure
tables.markdown:
| Composition | Low VOC, Waterbased, NON-DG |
| --- | --- |
| Certification | BS476, Part 21 \| UL 263 \| GB14907-2018 |
| Building Classification | C1 and C2 Environments |
| Specific Gravity | 1.30 ± 0.01kg/L (25oC) |
| Volume Solids | 69% ± 3% |
| VOC | 1 g/L |
| Theoretical Coverage | 1800g/m² based on an applied @ 1.0mm DFT |
| Colour | White / matte |
| Primer | Epoxy Zinc Primer or similar |
| Tocoat | Water-based topcoats (interior if required) |
| Drying Time at 25oC | 2 hours (touch) 4 hours (hard dry) 72 hours full cure |
| Coating Intervals at 25oC | Spray min. 4-6 hours |

Disclaimer: The above data, particularly the recommendations for the application and use of Charcoat Passive Fire Protection products are
based on the manufacturer’s knowledge and experience. Due to different materials and conditions of application, which are beyond our
control, we recommend in any case to carry out sufficient tests in order to ensure that Charcoat Passive Fire Protection products are
suitable for the intended purpose and applications. Therefore, any liability for such recommendations or any oral advice is expressly
excluded unless we have acted willfully or by gross negligence. It is always the responsibility of the installer / purchaser to guarantee
correct preparation, DFT (Charcoat Coatings) and thickness (Charcoat Firestop Products) of all Charcoat Passive Fire Protection
products. Charcoat Passive Fire Protection is not liable for installation or faulty installation. It is always the responsibility of the
inst aller / purchaser to guarantee and certify the installation of materials.
